channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more Israeli hostages in latest exchange
item.description: NUSEIRAT, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas has handed over three more Israeli hostages to the Red Cross in the latest exchange under the fragile ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Illinois man indicted in teenage stepson's murder after alleged torture, beating
item.description: An Illinois man is facing murder charges in connection with allegations that prolonged abuse and torture led to the death of his 16-year-old stepson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Murder charge issued in death of Michigan 17-year-old
item.description: A murder charge has been filed in the death of a 17-year-old at a Holland, Michigan, apartment early Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Corinne Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Fired employees fear beloved Yosemite National Park will lose its luster
item.description: YOSEMITE NATIONAL PARK, Calif. (AP) — Yosemite National Park employees who recently were fired by the Trump administration worry cuts will affect the experience of visitors and the welfare of wildlife that thrive in the popular vacation destination located in California’s Sierra Nevada.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is being buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group is scheduled to be buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ceiba Phillips, 11, couldn't believe what he saw when he returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after a wildfire reduced it to rubble. The ruins of his best friend’s house and his beloved school.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: White House and Ukraine nearing rare earths deal that would tighten relationship, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House and Ukraine have made significant progress toward reaching an agreement that would provide the U.S. with access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals and tighten the long-term relationship between Kyiv and Washington, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US offers UN resolution on war in Ukraine that stops far short of competing European statement
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States has proposed a draft U.N. resolution that stops far short of a competing European-backed statement demanding an immediate withdrawal of all of Moscow’s forces from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked parts of President Trump’s executive orders that sought to end the federal government’s backing of diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) efforts.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KREX 5

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Six weeks after the start of the most destructive wildfire in city history, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the fire chief Friday amid a public rift over preparations for a potential blaze and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall over responsibility for the devastat
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 shot and killed outside Louisville, Kentucky, motor vehicle office, police say
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Three people were shot and killed outside a motor vehicle office in Louisville on Friday, police said.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge clears path to pull USAID personnel off the job
item.description: A Trump-appointed judge on Friday greenlit the administration’s plans to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on administrative leave and recall many from field offices around the world. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Spring break warning: Most unlikely American tourist hot spots emerge as deadly risk
item.description: The Caribbean, a popular spring break destination for Americans, is relatively safe, but the State Department is warning U.S. tourists to stay away from certain countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: Judge declines to halt Trump administration's federal workers layoffs
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday refused to temporarily block the Trump administration’s mass layoff of federal workers while a lawsuit brought by five unions moves forward. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration can move ahead on mass government layoffs
item.description: A federal judge Thursday refused a group of government employee unions’ request to block the Trump administration from moving ahead with plans to dramatically reduce the federal workforce. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: That marks a break from President Trump and tech billionaire and Trump adviser Elon Musk, who have floated the idea in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Kids' disability rights cases stalled as Trump began to overhaul Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It was obvious to Christine Smith Olsey that her son was not doing well at school, despite educators telling her to leave it to the experts. The second-grade student stumbled over words, and other kids teased him so much he started to call himself “an idiot.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump media company sues a Brazilian Supreme Court justice investigating Bolsonaro
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump's media company sued a Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Wednesday, accusing him of violating U.S. free speech protections when he imposed a ban on a right-wing supporter of the country's former President Jair Bolsonaro.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month into second term: Survey
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating appears to be underwater as many suggest he may be overstepping his authority, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls frozen item that 'may contain' plastic, removes product from shelves
item.description: A representative for Trader Joe’s told Nexstar on Thursday that the frozen product is not currently available at stores amid the recall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: 2 students arrested for allegedly plotting 'mass casualty attack' at Houston school, FBI says
item.description: Two high school students have been arrested for planning what authorities are calling a “mass casualty attack” at a Houston high school.
item.dc:creator: Chad Washington, KRON4

item.title: McConnell won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) announced Thursday that he is not seeking reelection in 2026, bringing an end to the longest-serving Senate leader’s political career.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KRON4

item.title: Creative control of James Bond franchise given to Amazon MGM
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios and longtime Bond custodians Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli formed a new joint venture in which they will co-own James Bond intellectual property rights — but Amazon MGM will have creative control.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, KRON4

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: USDA working to rescind terminations for fired officials working on bird flu efforts
item.description: The accidental firings come amid the Trump administration’s sweeping push to reduce the size of the government and cut the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brutal polar vortex cold shutters Midwest schools, breaks North Dakota record
item.description: More than 95 million people are facing gripping cold Tuesday as a polar vortex sends temperatures plunging to record levels, closing schools, bursting pipes and forcing communities to set up more temporary shelters for the homeless.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: JFK Presidential Library closes amid Trump federal worker culling
item.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum closed on Tuesday and will remain closed until further notice, a notice from its foundation read.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: US Catholic bishops sue Trump administration for halt in funding for refugee settlement
item.description: Catholic bishops sued the Trump administration on Tuesday over its abrupt halt to funding of refugee resettlement, calling the action unlawful and harmful to newly arrived refugees and to the nation's largest private resettlement program. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, WGN 9
